author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXXVI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …How man had come into captivity, let us ask the Apostle Paul.…For he saith: “For we know that the Law is spiritual, but I am carnal, sold under sin.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold whence we became captives; because we were sold under sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who sold us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We ourselves, who consented to the seducer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We could sell ourselves; we could not redeem ourselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We sold ourselves by consent of sin, we are redeemed in the faith of righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For innocent blood was given for us, that we might be redeemed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatsoever blood he shed in persecuting the righteous, what kind of blood did he shed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Righteous men’s blood, indeed, he shed; they were Prophets, righteous men, our fathers, and Martyrs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whose blood he shed, yet all coming of the offspring of sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One blood he shed of Him who was not justified, but born righteous: by shedding that blood, he lost those whom he held.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they for whom innocent blood was given were redeemed, and, turned back from their captivity, they sing this Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When the Lord turned back the captivity of Sion, we became as those that are comforted” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He meant by this to say, we became joyful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When the Lord turned back the captivity of Sion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is Sion?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Jerusalem, the same is also the eternal Sion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How is Sion eternal, how is Sion captive?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In angels eternal, in men captive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not all the citizens of that city are captives, but those who are away from thence, they are captives.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Man was a citizen of Jerusalem, but sold under sin he became a pilgrim.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of his progeny was born the human race, and the captivity of Sion filled all lands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how is this captivity of Sion a shadow of that Jerusalem?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The shadow of that Sion, which was granted to the Jews, in an image, in a figure, was in captivity in Babylonia, and after seventy years that people turned back to its own city.…But when all time is past, then we return to our country, as after seventy years that people returned from the Babylonish captivity, for Babylon is this world; since Babylon is interpreted “confusion.”…So then this whole life of human affairs is confusion, which belongeth not unto God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this confusion, in this Babylonish land, Sion is held captive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “the Lord hath turned back the captivity of Sion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And we became,” he saith, “as those that are comforted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, we rejoiced as receiving consolation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consolation is not save for the unhappy, consolation is not save for them that groan, that mourn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore, “as those that are comforted,” except because we are still mourning?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We mourn for our present lot, we are comforted in hope: when the present is passed by, of our mourning will come everlasting joy, when there will be no need of consolation, because we shall be wounded with no distress.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wherefore saith he “as” those that are comforted, and saith not comforted?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This word “as,” is not always put for likeness: when we say “As,” it sometimes refers to the actual case, sometimes to likeness: here it is with reference to the actual case.…Walk therefore in Christ, and sing rejoicing, sing as one that is comforted; because He went before thee who hath commanded thee to follow Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Then was our mouth filled with joy, and our tongue with exultation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That mouth, brethren, which we have in our body, how is it “filled with joy”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It useth not to be “filled,” save with meat, or drink, or some such thing put into the mouth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes our mouth is filled; and it is more that we say to your holiness, when we have our mouth full, we cannot speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we have a mouth within, that is, in the heart, whence whatsoever proceedeth, if it is evil, defileth us, if it is good, cleanseth us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For concerning this very mouth ye heard when the Gospel was read.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Jews reproached the Lord, because His disciples ate with unwashen hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They reproached who had cleanness without; and within were full of stains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They reproached, whose righteousness was only in the eyes of men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the Lord sought our inward cleanness, which if we have, the outside must needs be clean also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Cleanse,” He saith, “the inside,” and “the outside shall be clean also.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let us return to what was just now read from the Gospel, relating to the verse before us, “Our mouth was filled with joy, and our tongue with delight:” for we are inquiring what mouth and what tongue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen, beloved brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord was scoffed at, because His disciples ate with unwashed hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord answered them as was fitting, and said unto the crowds whom He had called unto Him, “Hear ye all, and understand: not that which goeth into the mouth defileth a man; but that which cometh out of the mouth, this defileth a man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this? when He said, what goeth into the mouth, He meant only the mouth of the body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For meat goeth in, and meats defile not a man; because, “All things are clean to the clean;” and, “every creature of God is good, and none to be refused, if it be received with thanksgiving.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Guard the mouth of thy heart from evil, and thou wilt be innocent: the tongue of thy body will be innocent, thy hands will be innocent; even thy feet will be innocent, thy eyes, thy ears, will be innocent; all thy members will serve under righteousness, because a righteous commander hath thy heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Then shall they say among the heathen, the Lord hath done great things for them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Yea, the Lord hath done great things for us already, whereof we rejoice” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider, my brethren, if Sion doth not at present say this among the heathen, throughout the whole world; consider if men are not running unto the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the whole world our redemption is received; Amen is answered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The dwellers in Jerusalem, therefore, captive, destined to return, pilgrims, sighing for their country, speak thus among the heathen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do they say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord hath done great things for us, whereof we rejoice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have they done anything for themselves?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have done ill with themselves, for they have sold themselves under sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Redeemer came, and did the good things for them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Turn our captivity, O Lord, as the torrents in the south” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider, my brethren, what this meaneth.…As torrents are turned in the south, so turn our captivity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In a certain passage Scripture saith, in admonishing us concerning good works, “Thy sins also shall melt away, even as the ice in fair warm weather.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our sins therefore bound us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As the cold bindeth the water that it run not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Bound with the frost of our sins, we have frozen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the south wind is a warm wind: when the south wind blows, the ice melts, and the torrents are filled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now winter streams are called torrents; for filled with sudden rains they run with great force.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We had therefore become frozen in captivity; our sins bound us: the south wind the Holy Spirit hath blown: our sins are forgiven us, we are released from the frost of iniquity; as the ice in fair weather, our sins are melted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us run unto our country, as the torrents in the south.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the next words are, “They that sow in tears, shall reap in joy” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this life, which is full of tears, let us sow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What shall we sow?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Works of mercy are our seeds: of which seeds the Apostle saith, “Let us not be weary in well doing; for in due season we shall reap if we faint not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Speaking therefore of almsgiving itself, what saith he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This I say; he that soweth sparingly, shall reap also sparingly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore who soweth plentifully, shall reap plentifully: he who soweth sparingly, shall reap also sparingly: and he that soweth nothing, shall reap nothing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do ye long for ample estates, where ye may sow plentifully?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is not a wider field on which ye can sow than Christ, who hath willed that we should sow in Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Your soil is the Church; sow as much as ye can.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou hast not enough to do this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hast thou the will?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As what thou hadst would be nothing, if thou hadst not a good will; so do not despond, because thou hast not, if thou hast a good will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what dost thou sow?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what wilt thou reap?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Said the Angels, Peace on earth unto rich men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No, but, “Peace on earth unto men of a good will.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Zacchæus had a strong will, Zacchæus had great charity.…Did then that widow who cast her two farthings into the treasury, sow little?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, as much as Zacchæus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For she had narrower means, but an equal will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She gave her two mites with as good a will as Zacchæus gave the half of his patrimony.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou consider what they gave, thou wilt find their gifts different; if thou look to the source, thou wilt find them equal; she gave whatever she had, and he gave what he had.…But if they are beggars whose profession is asking alms, in trouble they also have what to bestow upon one another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God hath not so forsaken them, but that they have wherein they may be tried by their bestowing of alms.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This man cannot walk; he who can walk, lendeth his feet to the lame; he who seeth, lendeth his eyes to the blind; and he who is young and sound, lendeth his strength to the old or the infirm, carrieth him: the one is poor, the other is rich.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes also the rich man is found to be poor, and something is bestowed upon him by the poor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Somebody cometh to a river, so much the more delicate as he is more rich; he cannot pass over: if he were to pass over with bare limbs, he would catch cold, would be ill, would die: a poor man more active in body cometh up: he carries the rich man over; he giveth alms unto the rich.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think not therefore those only poor, who have not money.…Thus love ye, thus be ye affectioned unto one another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Attend not solely to yourselves: but to those who are in want around you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because these things take place in this life with troubles and cares, faint not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye sow in tears, ye shall reap in joy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How, my brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the farmer goeth forth with the plough, carrying seed, is not the wind sometimes keen, and doth not the shower sometimes deter him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He looketh to the sky, seeth it lowering, shivers with cold, nevertheless goeth forth, and soweth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he feareth lest while he is observing the foul weather, and awaiting sunshine, the time may pass away, and he may not find anything to reap.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Put not off, my brethren; sow in wintry weather, sow good works, even while ye weep; for, “They that sow in tears, shall reap in joy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They sow their seed, good will, and good works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They went on their way and wept, casting their seed” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why did they weep?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they were among the miserable, and were themselves miserable.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is better, my brethren, that no man should be miserable, than that thou shouldest do alms.…Nevertheless, as long as there are objects for its exercise, let us not fail amid those troubles to sow our seed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although we sow in tears, yet shall we reap in joy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in that resurrection of the dead, each man shall receive his own sheaves, that is, the produce of his seed, the crown of joys and of delight.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then will there be a joyous triumph, when we shall laugh at death, wherein we groaned before: then shall they say to death, “O death, where is thy strife?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O death, where is thy sting?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But why do they now rejoice?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “they bring their sheaves with them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this Psalm we have chiefly exhorted you to do deeds of alms, because it is thence that we ascend; and ye see that he who ascendeth, singeth the song of steps.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Remember: do not love to descend, instead of to ascend, but reflect upon your ascent: because he who descended from Jerusalem to Jericho fell among thieves.…The Samaritan as He passed by slighted us not: He healed us, He raised us upon His beast, upon His flesh; He led us to the inn, that is, the Church; He entrusted us to the host, that is, to the Apostle; He gave two pence, whereby we might be healed, the love of God, and the love of our neighbour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostle spent more; for, though it was allowed unto all the Apostles to receive, as Christ’s soldiers, pay from Christ’s subjects, that Apostle, nevertheless, toiled with his own hands, and excused the subjects the maintenance owing to him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All this hath already happened: if we have descended, and have been wounded; let us ascend, let us sing, and make progress, in order that we may arrive.
